details: The npm package tailwind-core versions 4.3.1 and 4.3.2 contain remote code execution payloads hidden in the exported Tailwind-style runtime. The package root export reaches code that fingerprints the host, communicates with attacker-controlled Google Apps Script/Drive or Sheets resources, decodes remote instructions, and executes returned code through Function/AsyncFunction constructors. This behavior is unrelated to a Tailwind CSS utility package and exposes consuming projects to remote code execution and host/environment disclosure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code

cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of a code segment using externally-influenced input from an upstream component.
cwes.name: Improper Control of Generation of Code

cwes.description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.
cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
